missing_external_entities.title: {count} required entities are missing
missing_external_entities.description:
The area_lighting integration expects the following entities to exist in Home Assistant, but they are missing:

{missing_list}

Without these, features depending on them are disabled or degraded (holiday detection, ambient zones, motion lighting, circadian sensor readings).

### Copy-paste bootstrap

Add this to your `configuration.yaml` (or a package file) to create the missing helpers:

```yaml
{bootstrap_yaml}
```

After saving the file, reload the affected integrations or restart Home Assistant. See the component README for full setup details.

scene_drift_unhealable.title: {area}: a light keeps drifting from its scene
scene_drift_unhealable.description: Home Assistant repeatedly tried to restore `{entity}` in {area} to its active scene, but it keeps changing back on its own. This usually means a Philips Hue power-on default, a Zigbee/RF dropout, or a Hue-app automation is driving the bulb independently of Home Assistant. Check the bulb's power-on behavior (set it to 'last state') and disable any competing Hue motion/automation for this room. The area has been left in `manual` until the next motion or remote event.
